title: A。そういえば B。
titleEn: Recalling something related to A; speaking of which, by the way, now that you mention it
explanation: Dùng khi từ điều vừa nói ở A, người nói chợt nhớ hoặc chuyển sang một thông tin liên quan ở B. Sắc thái gần với “nhắc mới nhớ”, “nói mới nhớ”, “nhân tiện”. Cấu trúc: A + そういえば + B.
explanationEn: The A。そういえば B。grammar point is used when something related to A is recalled or brought up. It can be translated as speaking of which, by the way, or now that you mention it in English. It is commonly used to connect two related topics or ideas in a conversation and indicates a change of focus. The formation consists of two clauses: the first being A and the second being B, with そういえば in between as a connector
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: A。そこで B。
titleEn: Expresses therefore or because of that
explanation: Dùng khi A nêu tình huống, vấn đề hoặc lý do, còn B là hành động, quyết định hoặc kết quả được đưa ra vì tình huống đó. Có thể hiểu là “vì thế”, “do đó”. Cấu trúc: A + そこで + B.
explanationEn: The A。そこで B。grammar point is used to describe the reason for an action or situation in the A part and the result or decision made in the B part. It can be translated as therefore or because of that in English
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: A。それがB。
titleEn: Expressing thats exactly what A is; in other words, B
structure: A (Statement)。それが B (Restatement or explanation of A)。
structureEn: A (Statement)。それが B (Restatement or explanation of A)。
explanation: Dùng để làm rõ rằng điểm cốt lõi, thực chất hoặc điều đáng nói của A chính là B. Mẫu này thường mang sắc thái giải thích lại hoặc nhấn mạnh bản chất của sự việc. Cấu trúc: A (Statement)。それが B (Restatement or explanation of A)。.
explanationEn: The grammar point A。それがB。can be used to restate or clarify that B is precisely what A means. It emphasizes the essence or definition of A by stating B. It can be translated as that’s what A is, that is precisely A/B, or in other words, B
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: A。それで B。
titleEn: Express a sequence of events or cause-and-effect; and then, so, because of that
explanation: Dùng để nối hai sự việc theo trình tự hoặc quan hệ nguyên nhân-kết quả: A xảy ra, rồi B xảy ra; hoặc vì A nên B. Có thể dịch là “rồi”, “vì vậy”, “thế là”. Cấu trúc: Sentence A +。それで+ Sentence B。.
explanationEn: The A。それで B。grammar point is used to connect two sentences or events, expressing a sequence of events or explaining the reason for the second event based on the first event. Essentially, it means and then, so, or because of that in English
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: A。それでも B。
titleEn: Expresses even so, nevertheless, still, or but; used to contrast two statements
structure: A (situation) + 。それでも + B (action or thought)
structureEn: A (situation) + 。それでも + B (action or thought)
explanation: Dùng khi B vẫn xảy ra hoặc vẫn đúng dù A là một tình huống trái chiều. Sắc thái nhấn mạnh sự nhượng bộ: “dù vậy”, “thế nhưng vẫn”. Cấu trúc: A (situation) + 。それでも + B (action or thought).
explanationEn: The A。それでも B。grammar point is used to show a contrast between two statements. The first statement (A) presents a situation, and the second statement (B) presents an action or thought that might be unexpected or contrary to the initial situation. It can be translated as even so, nevertheless, still, or but in English
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: A。それなのに B。
titleEn: Express contrast or a contradiction between two situations or facts; even though, despite, but still
explanation: Dùng để chỉ sự trái ngược hoặc kết quả ngoài dự đoán: A là sự thật, thế mà B lại xảy ra khác với điều người nói mong đợi. Có thể dịch là “vậy mà”, “thế nhưng”. Cấu trúc: A。それなのに B。.
explanationEn: The A。それなのにB。grammar point is used to express contrast, contradiction or unexpectedness between two situations or facts. It can be translated as even though, despite, or but still in English. The formation is simple, with A and B representing the two contrasting or contradicting statements
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: A。それなら B。
titleEn: Express if thats the case, then; used to show a logical consequence or decision based on a given situation
structure: Situation A (sentence) + 。それなら + Situation B (sentence)
structureEn: Situation A (sentence) + 。それなら + Situation B (sentence)
explanation: Dùng khi dựa trên tình huống A để đưa ra quyết định, đề xuất hoặc kết luận B. Có thể hiểu là “nếu vậy thì”, “trong trường hợp đó”. Cấu trúc: Situation A (sentence) + 。それなら + Situation B (sentence).
explanationEn: The A。それなら B。grammar point is used to express a logical consequence or decision based on the given situation in the first part of the sentence (A). The phrase それなら can be translated as if thats the case, in that case, or then in English
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: A それはそうと B。
titleEn: Changing the topic; by the way, speaking of which
structure: Sentence A + それはそうと + Sentence B
structureEn: Sentence A + それはそうと + Sentence B
explanation: Dùng để gác lại nội dung A và chuyển sang chủ đề B. Sắc thái hội thoại, gần với “chuyện đó để sau”, “nhân tiện nói sang chuyện khác”. Cấu trúc: Sentence A + それはそうと + Sentence B.
explanationEn: The grammar point A それはそうと B is used to change the topic or bring up a new subject in a conversation. It can be translated as by the way or speaking of which in English. Its used to connect two unrelated sentences or ideas, with A being the original topic and B being the new topic brought up
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)
